rationale_for_baseline_tier: Cutaneous periocular HSV with the eye currently uninvolved needs prompt antivirals and urgent (next-day) in-person/ophthalmic review because keratitis cannot be excluded remotely; escalate to T4 immediately for any ocular symptoms.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Red/painful eye, light sensitivity, gritty/foreign-body feeling, blurred vision, or vesicles on the eyelid margin/nose tip
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Attend an eye emergency department urgently
escalation_edges.rationale: Signals possible herpetic keratitis/ocular involvement — vision-threatening.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If your eye becomes red or painful, sensitive to light, feels gritty, or your vision blurs — or blisters appear on your eyelid edge or nose tip — go to an eye emergency department straight away.

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Ensure clear written ocular safety-net and confirm the patient can attend urgent review.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Start the antiviral tablets now and get your eye checked in person tomorrow. Wash your hands well, don't touch your eye or the blisters, and avoid makeup/contact lenses on that side. Go to an eye emergency if your eye becomes red, painful, light-sensitive, gritty, or your vision blurs.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Urgent next-day GP/ophthalmology; eye emergency department for any ocular symptoms
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: Attend an eye emergency department after hours if any ocular symptoms develop.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Document periocular cutaneous HSV, antivirals commenced, ocular screen negative, and request urgent slit-lamp/fluorescein review.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Reassuring as a simple cold sore with no antivirals, ocular screen, safety-net or eye review.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: Herpetic keratitis develops undetected and causes corneal scarring and permanent vision loss.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Routine handling that delays antivirals and the urgent eye review.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Calling an ambulance when urgent next-day eye review/eye-ED suffices in the absence of current ocular symptoms.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Cannot perform a slit-lamp/fluorescein exam remotely, so herpetic keratitis cannot be excluded.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Start antivirals, advise strict hygiene, arrange urgent in-person eye review, and give a strong ocular safety-net rather than remote reassurance.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Telling the patient the eye is fine without an exam, or delaying antivirals/review.
